channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The Vance Peace Deal
item.description: The U.S. vice president has become the face of an unpopular deal.

item.title: Israeli Officials and U.S. Lawmakers Slam Iran Deal
item.description: The MOU is the “worst foreign policy blunder in decades,” Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy wrote.

item.title: Ordinary Iranians Won’t See a Dime of Trump’s Money
item.description: As the public suffers, a corrupt regime prepares for a bonanza.

item.title: The Era of the French Intellectual Is Over
item.description: The recently deceased Edgar Morin was the last reminder of a bygone phenomenon.

item.title: Will Colombia Elect Its Own Bukele?
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella promises megaprisons and more if he wins the presidency.

item.title: How the Iran War Reshaped the Global Landscape of Power
item.description: The United States and Israel will be weaker for years to come.

item.title: The Iran Deal Is Hazy on Hormuz
item.description: Everything is clear except what will happen to shipping and oil exports.

item.title: A Brief History of Political Trolling
item.description: What was once a fringe style of provocation is now a ubiquitous weapon.

item.title: The Real Reason Nothing Works in Britain
item.description: Two new books hint at what’s actually beneath U.K. political and economic malaise.

item.title: The G7 has nudged open a window for diplomacy in Ukraine
item.description: Europeans see a hopeful shift in America’s attitude

item.title: The Meaning of Limited Government—Then and Now
item.description: In 1776, Adam Smith and America’s founders envisioned an economy that allowed individuals to pursue their own material well-being, with minimal government intervention. Today, America’s Republican Party claims to be committed to restoring that laissez-faire ideal, even as they make a mockery of its animating principles.

item.title: No Reset at G7: Trump Was Trump, Modi Was Not Modi
item.description: The Modi-Trump meeting revealed the limits of India’s willingness to speak with moral clarity when confronted with American power.

item.title: Xi’s Authoritarian Blueprint for the Myanmar Junta
item.description: Xi implied to Min Aung Hlaing that brute military force is failing, and he must instead master CCP-style totalitarian controls to pacify the public.

item.title: The Iran War Damaged U.S.-India Ties
item.description: Diplomatic fractures between the two strategic partners are likely to outlast the conflict.

item.title: Floating Volcanic Rock Is Disrupting Life in Papua New Guinea
item.description: The ongoing Titan Ridge eruption has left parts of PNG’s coast covered in meters of floating volcanic rock. For a community dependent on the ocean, that's a serious problem.

item.title: AI Sovereignty Is About Options, Not Ownership
item.description: The US government’s abrupt decision to suspend foreign access to Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models clarifies what “AI sovereignty” is really about. In the emerging AI economy, competitive advantage will come not from owning a single model, but from being able to evaluate, select, and orchestrate many models.

item.title: Wang Yi’s Visit and Mongolia’s Foreign Policy Balancing Act
item.description: How does the Chinese foreign minister’s trip intersect with Mongolia’s hosting of the U.N. desertification conference and its broader “third neighbor” policy?

item.title: The Promise and Peril of AGI
item.description: Artificial general intelligence could dramatically increase human prosperity and free people from countless mundane tasks. But unless its benefits and ownership are broadly shared, it could deprive billions of workers of their livelihoods and pave the way for a new form of techno-authoritarianism.

item.title: The United States Has a Kafala Problem
item.description: Qatar’s World Cup became known for migrant worker abuse. U.S. labor systems are similarly exploitative.

item.title: The USMCA Review Will Be a China (and Asia) Policy Test for Mexico
item.description: Washington wants Mexico to prove it is not a backdoor for China. But Mexico’s factories still depend on Asian inputs.

item.title: Why the 4-Inch ‘Dancing Girl’ Figurine Rattles South Asia’s Puritans
item.description: The image of the nude torso of a millennia-old iconic figurine of a young woman was shaded in Indian school textbooks to make her appear clothed.

item.title: Nice: France’s Mediterranean Gateway to the Indo-Pacific
item.description: France’s fifth-largest city is increasingly serving as a showcase for Indo-Pacific diplomacy -- including the Indian PM's latest visit.

item.title: On the Bangladesh-India Border, BJP Victories Spike Anxieties
item.description: Since the BJP takeover of West Bengal, Muslims and Rohingyas in the region talk among themselves in hushed tones and try not to attract attention, worried about their fate.

item.title: Anthropic is battling Uncle Sam for control of superpowered AI
item.description: The struggle may end up denting its astonishing commercial success

item.title: Don’t restrict Chinese biotech
item.description: Patients benefit from faster, cheaper treatments, wherever they are invented

item.title: Britain is not yet ready to rejoin the EU
item.description: After a lost decade, it is time to focus on the future

item.title: AI has granted America vast new power
item.description: Its government is now the gatekeeper to frontier models—and most compute

item.title: From Philippine staple to global sensation: the rise of ube
item.description: The country is struggling to keep up with soaring demand

item.title: Taiwan’s opposition leader faced a tough crowd in America
item.description: Many still have doubts about her outreach to Xi Jinping

item.title: Myanmar’s junta revives a hated dam to crush dissent and court China
item.description: A dam halted by protests in 2011 is back, but not for the energy it will produce

item.title: The real winner in Myanmar’s civil war is China
item.description: It doesn’t want to fix the war-torn country but is fine managing it

item.title: News extortion is rife in China
item.description: The country’s censors may be making it worse

item.title: How China still outworks the West
item.description: A medal scheme masks the identity of its toughest labourers

item.title: Meet the world’s new peacemakers
item.description: Autocracies are taking over conflict mediation. The deals they are making look different

item.title: The left is coming for Democratic incumbents
item.description: In primary challenges, Mayor Zohran Mamdani is trying to install allies in Congress

item.title: Pet-custody laws in America are changing
item.description: More states are weighing pets’ interests, not just their price

item.title: Populist president, meet socialist mayor
item.description: America’s capital is poised to elect a new leftist leader

item.title: The weekly cartoon
Business
Politics
How plants keep tabs on the competition
item.description: They grow faster when their rivals are doing the same

item.title: Europeans should learn to love the air-conditioner
item.description: Green electricity means never having to say sorry for lowering the thermostat

item.title: Britain’s departure made Europe more French
item.description: A smaller EU has learned to live without Britain

item.title: How to launch a tech product
item.description: Create a slick, irony-free corporate safe space

item.title: The Iran war has boosted Equinor, Norway’s energy giant
item.description: But it cannot turn a greying cash cow into a sprightly upstart

item.title: Is the staple meal in Nigeria and Ghana becoming a luxury?
item.description: What the rising prices of jollof rice show about Nigeria and Ghana’s economies

item.title: The Iran war meant an economic crisis for Africa
item.description: It was the third shock of the 2020s to hit the continent

item.title: A flimsy deal will stop the bombing and restart the oil
item.description: Whether it can do anything more will be the question that defines the next few months

item.title: The Colombian diaspora is overwhelmingly right-wing
item.description: But not in Russia

item.title: The Gen-Z streamer from Medellín influencing Colombia’s election
item.description: Algorithms, not debates, will determine the country’s next president

item.title: Myanmar’s junta revives a hated dam to court China
item.description: A dam halted by protests in 2011 is back, but not for the energy it will produce

item.title: Central Asia’s Relations With Taliban-ruled Afghanistan Continue to Deepen
item.description: In April, the first Afghanistan-Central Asia Consultative Dialogue was held in Kabul, highlighting the considerable development of relations over the last five years.

item.title: Reversing Brexit Is Labour’s Best Hope
item.description: When it comes to the relationship between the United Kingdom and the European Union, political expediency and economic logic both point in the same direction. A majority of British voters now believe that Brexit was a mistake, and they are looking for a leader who will put the issue back on the national agenda.

item.title: Fragmented Trade and the Failure of Sanctioned Oil Isolation
item.description: The geopolitics behind how sanctioned oil continues moving through parallel trading networks despite efforts to curb its flow and what this means for global trading hubs like Singapore

item.title: Global Health Reform Must Bolster Innovation
item.description: As funding falls for global public health, calls for a country-led system have grown louder. Such reforms are necessary, but they must not come at the expense of new vaccines, medicines, and diagnostics, which can be delivered by strengthening mechanisms—such as product development partnerships—that already work.

item.title: War has strengthened the Islamic Republic. Peace could split it
item.description: For now, the hardliners are in the ascendant

item.title: A new golden age for Japanese banks comes with a catch
item.description: Smaller lenders are saddled with low-return bonds they cannot sell

item.title: Europe buys the future, America builds it
item.description: Does that matter? A high-stakes transatlantic spat has erupted over GDP figures

item.title: Tata’s big bets are yet to pay off
item.description: The Indian conglomerate’s boardroom drama highlights a speculative strategy

item.title: Republicans are desperate to move on from the Iran war
item.description: The conflict will have stubborn political after-effects

item.title: Ireland Is Becoming a French Military Protectorate
item.description: Literally defenseless, Dublin is outsourcing security to Paris.

item.title: India’s new economy still faces an old problem
item.description: Family-run conglomerates make the stock market a tricky place to invest

item.title: Donald Trump gambles that Iran wants money more than power
item.description: The peace deal is all carrot and no stick

item.title: Philippines Confirms Removal of Floating Platform at Scarborough Shoal
item.description: China deployed the platform at the disputed atoll late last month, for what it described as a program of "comprehensive research" in the South China Sea.

item.title: A Diplomatic Row Roils India-Bangladesh Relations Yet Again
item.description: While Delhi’s ties with Dhaka are hit by one hiccup after another, Bangladesh's PM Tarique Rahman is set to visit China next week.

item.title: India’s 5G Fighter Aircraft Dilemma
item.description: Russia’s recent offer to India for joint production of the SU-57 fifth-generation fighter comes amid India’s own AMCA project taking some definitive steps forward.

item.title: Brunei Pumps More Oil
Multilateralism Is Dead. Long Live Plurilateralism.
item.description: Coalitions of the willing are creating a “race to the top” on climate change, AI, and critical minerals.

item.title: China Is Pulling Up the Ladder Behind It
item.description: How Beijing’s export strategy will keep poor countries poor.

item.title: Iran Won the War but May Lose the Peace
item.description: Tehran is poised to overplay its hand.

item.title: How France Falls to the Far Right
item.description: The growing odds and seismic consequences of a Le Pen-Bardella victory.

item.title: Philippine Senate Votes to Remove Duterte Ally From Chamber’s Presidency
item.description: The elevation of Senator Sherwin Gatchalian comes just weeks before the chamber is set to begin the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte.

item.title: China Is Indispensable, But Not Dominant
item.description: China has drawn the great powers into its orbit without controlling their strategic choices.
